tasks.title: Minimal fx hello world
tasks.prompt: Write the smallest valid Aether program that prints exactly `hello from benchmark` followed by a newline.

tasks.title: Constants plus fx output
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program that defines `Greeting` as `hello` and `Target` as `Aether`, then prints exactly `hello, Aether` followed by a newline.

tasks.title: Pure helper plus effectful output
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program with a pure helper `classify(score: Int) -> Text` that returns `ready` for scores >= 90, `review` for scores >= 70, and `blocked` otherwise. In `main`, print three lines for scores 95, 72, and 10 in the format `95 => ready`.

tasks.title: Type block with self mutation
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program that defines a `Counter` type with a `value: Int` field and a `bump() -> Int` method that increments the field and returns it. In `main`, create a counter, initialize it correctly, call `bump()` twice, and print each returned value on its own line.

tasks.title: Real arithmetic with formatted output
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program with `const PI: Real = 3.14159265;` and a pure helper `area(radius: Real) -> Real`. Use radius `2.5` and print exactly `area=19.63` followed by a newline. Use Aether's real formatting syntax instead of string post-processing.

tasks.title: Inline conditional expression
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program that computes `avg0` from `total0 = 9` and `count0 = 0`, and `avg2` from `total2 = 9` and `count2 = 2`, using inline `if ... else ...` expressions on the right-hand side. Print `avg0=0` and `avg2=4` on separate lines.

tasks.title: String length and equality checks
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program with a pure helper `validName(name: Text) -> Bool` that returns true only when the name length is between 1 and 6 inclusive and the name is not equal to `bad`. In `main`, print `alpha=true`, `empty=false`, and `bad=false` on separate lines for the sample inputs `alpha`, ``, and `bad`.

tasks.title: Half-open range loop
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program that uses a `loop i in 0..5` style range loop and prints one line per iteration in the form `0 => 0`, `1 => 1`, ..., `4 => 16`.

tasks.title: Tuple return plus destructuring
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program with a helper `pair() -> (Text, Int)` that returns `("answer", 42)`. Destructure the direct call with `let (label, value) = pair();` and print `answer 42` followed by a newline.

tasks.title: Import provided module function
tasks.prompt: A module file named `bench_math` is provided in the working directory. Write an Aether program that imports it using current Aether import syntax, calls the exported helper that returns 42, and prints exactly `42` followed by a newline.

tasks.title: Import provided module constants
tasks.prompt: A module file named `bench_consts` is provided in the working directory. Write an Aether program that imports it using current Aether import syntax, reads the exported constants, and prints `Aether` on the first line and `42` on the second line.

tasks.title: Preconditions and postconditions
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program with `@pre` and `@post` contracts on a helper `normalize(score: Int) -> Int` that clamps a non-negative score into the range 0..100. Call it for 150 and 42, and print `150 => 100` and `42 => 42` on separate lines.

tasks.title: Record-style type initialization
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program that defines a `Point` type with `x` and `y` integer fields and a `sum() -> Int` method. Initialize a point with values 3 and 4 using current Aether type-initialization syntax, and print exactly `sum=7` followed by a newline.

tasks.title: TOON parsing with inline node lookup
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program that checks `has_toon()`. If TOON support is missing, print `toon unavailable` and exit. Otherwise parse the JSON text `{\"name\":\"Aether\",\"score\":42}`, extract the `name` and `score`, print them as `Aether 42`, close the document, and return.

tasks.title: TOON parse_file with typed extraction
tasks.prompt: A file named `payload.json` is provided in the working directory. Write an Aether program that checks `has_toon()`, parses the file with `toon_parse_file`, extracts `name`, `enabled`, and `count`, prints those three values on separate lines, closes the document, and returns.

tasks.title: TOON array iteration with classification summary
tasks.prompt: A file named `jobs.json` is provided in the working directory. Write an Aether program that checks `has_toon()`, parses the file, iterates through the `jobs` array, uses a pure classifier helper to map scores to `ready`, `review`, or `blocked`, prints one line per job in the form `job 0 => Alpha / ready / 95`, then prints total counts for `ready`, `review`, and `blocked`.
tasks.expected_stdout:
job 0 => Alpha / ready / 95
job 1 => Beta / review / 78
job 2 => Gamma / blocked / 62
total = 3
ready = 1
review = 1
blocked = 1

tasks.title: Safe nested TOON lookups with missing data
tasks.prompt: A file named `rows.json` is provided in the working directory. Write an Aether program that checks `has_toon()`, parses the file, iterates through the `rows` array, safely reads `meta.code` when present, falls back to `EMPTY` when it is absent, prints `row 0 = A1` style lines, counts how many rows resolved to `EMPTY`, and prints `missing = N` at the end. Use the safer staged TOON access pattern rather than assuming nested fallbacks protect the whole chain.
tasks.expected_stdout:
row 0 = A1
row 1 = EMPTY
row 2 = EMPTY
missing = 2

tasks.title: Dynamic array length and indexing
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program that creates an `Int[]` dynamic array containing the values 3, 1, 4, 1, and 5 using supported array syntax. Iterate over it with `length(...)` and indexing, compute the sum and maximum value, and print `sum=14`, `max=5`, and `count=5` on separate lines.

tasks.title: Real percentage formatting
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program that computes a success percentage from `ok = 3` and `total = 4`, forces real arithmetic correctly, and prints exactly `pct=75.00` followed by a newline using Aether's real-formatting syntax.

tasks.title: Stateful type with status method
tasks.prompt: Write an Aether program that defines an `Inventory` type with `currentStock` and `maxCapacity` fields, an `addItem(amount: Int) -> Int` method that updates the stock without exceeding capacity, and a `getStatus() -> Text` method that returns `FULL`, `IN_STOCK`, or `EMPTY`. Initialize the inventory to 10 of 50, add 25, and print `Final Stock: 35` and `Current Status: IN_STOCK` on separate lines.

tasks.title: Large agent-style report over TOON job data
tasks.prompt: A file named `agent_payload.json` is provided in the working directory. Write a clear, multi-helper Aether program that checks `has_toon()`, parses the file, normalizes scores into 0..100, classifies them as `ready`, `review`, or `blocked`, uses at least one custom `type` for structured intermediate data, prints one line per job in the form `job 0: Alpha / ready / 95`, then prints final totals. A clean answer will likely exceed 120 lines; do not golf it into one short function.
tasks.expected_stdout:
job 0: Alpha / ready / 95
job 1: Beta / review / 78
job 2: Gamma / blocked / 62
job 3: Delta / blocked / 41
job 4: Epsilon / ready / 100
total = 5
ready = 2
review = 1
blocked = 2

tasks.title: Large configuration validator with TOON and pure helpers
tasks.prompt: A file named `config.json` is provided in the working directory. Write a clear Aether program that checks `has_toon()`, parses the file, uses pure helpers to validate the port and application name, computes a numeric severity for the log level, prints one `ISSUE:` line for each validation problem, then prints the severity line and a final validation summary. A good answer should separate pure logic from `fx` output and use several helpers rather than a monolithic main function.
tasks.expected_stdout:
ISSUE: port is invalid (0)
ISSUE: app name is empty or too long
log severity level: 2
VALIDATION FAILED: 2 issue(s) found

tasks.title: Large ledger audit over nested transaction data
tasks.prompt: A file named `transactions.json` is provided in the working directory. Write a structured Aether program that checks `has_toon()`, parses the file, defines `const MaxTxAmount: Int = 10000;`, uses a pure helper `isTxValid(amount: Int) -> Bool`, maintains a custom `Ledger` type with counters, prints one line per transaction in the form `row 0: T1 / valid / 500`, and then prints batch summary lines for `batch`, `balance`, `valid`, and `invalid`. A clean answer will likely exceed 150 lines if written readably.
tasks.expected_stdout:
row 0: T1 / valid / 500
row 1: T2 / invalid / 99999
row 2: T3 / invalid / -100
row 3: UNKNOWN / invalid / -1
batch = batch_99
balance = 500
valid = 1
invalid = 3

tasks.title: Large release-board summary with normalization and averages
tasks.prompt: A file named `releases.json` is provided in the working directory. Write a structured Aether program that checks `has_toon()`, parses the file, normalizes scores into 0..100, classifies each release into `green`, `yellow`, or `red`, tracks whether each release is blocked based on `blockers > 0`, prints one line per release in the form `release 0: R1 / green / false / 92`, then prints the counts for each lane, the blocked count, and the average normalized score formatted to two decimals. Use several helpers and at least one custom `type`. A clean answer will likely exceed 150 lines.
tasks.expected_stdout:
release 0: R1 / green / false / 92
release 1: R2 / yellow / true / 76
release 2: R3 / red / false / 61
release 3: R4 / green / true / 100
green = 2
yellow = 1
red = 1
blocked = 2
average = 82.25

tasks.title: Large combined module plus TOON report
tasks.prompt: A module file named `bench_support` and a data file named `support_jobs.json` are provided in the working directory. Write a structured Aether program that imports the module, uses its exported default score and pure helpers, parses the job file, prints one line per job in the form `job 0 -> Alpha / ready / 95`, and then prints final totals for `ready`, `review`, and `blocked`. Keep the program cleanly separated into helpers, and prefer a readable multi-function solution over a compact one-liner.
tasks.expected_stdout:
job 0 -> Alpha / ready / 95
job 1 -> Beta / blocked / 50
job 2 -> Gamma / blocked / 12
ready = 1
review = 0
blocked = 2
